confirmed.title: table.md documents ImageColumn::circular() as taking a bool argument, but it takes none
confirmed.evidence: apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:36 lists the ImageColumn extra setter as `circular(bool)`. The source method in packages/table/src/Columns/ImageColumn.php:43 is `public function circular(): static` — it takes NO parameter (it is a no-arg toggle that sets shape to SHAPE_CIRCULAR; the opposite is the separate no-arg `square(): static` at line 50). A caller following the doc would write `->circular(true)` / `->circular(false)`, which is a TypeError against the real signature and there is no way to un-set circular via a bool. This same wrong signature `circular(bool)` is mirrored verbatim in the trilingual copies: apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/table.md:36 and apps/docs/es/reference/php/table.md:36.
confirmed.fix: Change `circular(bool)` to `circular()` (and optionally document `square()` as the inverse) in apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:36 and the pt-BR + es mirrors at the same line.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. Source packages/table/src/Columns/ImageColumn.php:43 declares `public function circular(): static` — it takes NO parameter; it is a no-arg toggle setting shape to SHAPE_CIRCULAR, with the separate no-arg `square(): static` (line 50) as the inverse. The docs at apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:36 (and the trilingual mirrors pt-BR/reference/php/table.md:36 and es/reference/php/table.md:36, all verified verbatim) document the setter as `circular(bool)`. This misleads a caller into writing `->circular(true)`/`->circular(false)`, implying a boolean can un-set the shape, which the API does not support (you must call `square()` instead). This is a genuine doc-vs-code contradiction. It is NOT covered by any SWEPT family: the R1-13 column-signature sweep explicitly enumerated only DateColumn/BadgeColumn/IconColumn/RelationshipColumn (and DateField displayFormat) — ImageColumn and its `circular()` method were never touched. Proposed fix: change `circular(bool)` to `circular()` (optionally note `square()` as inverse) at line 36 in all three locale files.

confirmed.title: core.md documents ResourceRegistry::discover() parameters in the wrong order
confirmed.evidence: apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:52 documents the method as `discover(string $namespace, string $path)`. The source in packages/core/src/Resources/ResourceRegistry.php:64 is `public function discover(string $path, string $namespace): void` — the parameters are in the REVERSED order ($path first, $namespace second). A caller following the doc would pass the namespace where the path is expected and vice-versa, breaking PSR-4 auto-discovery. The reversed-order doc is mirrored in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/core.md:52 and apps/docs/es/reference/php/core.md:52.
confirmed.fix: Change the documented signature to `discover(string $path, string $namespace)` in apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:52 and the pt-BR + es mirrors at the same line.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. Source packages/core/src/Resources/ResourceRegistry.php:64 declares `public function discover(string $path, string $namespace): void` — $path is first (used by is_dir($path) at line 66 and Finder->in($path) at line 72), $namespace is second (used for FQCN construction at line 76-81). The docs at apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:52 document the REVERSED order `discover(string $namespace, string $path)`, and this reversal is mirrored verbatim in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/core.md:52 and apps/docs/es/reference/php/core.md:52. A caller following the doc passes the namespace where the path is expected and vice-versa, breaking PSR-4 auto-discovery (is_dir() gets the namespace and returns early). This contradiction is NOT covered by any swept family (setters, Artisan lists, env vars, entry points, hooks, action/field methods); ResourceRegistry::discover() parameter order has never been touched. Fix: change the documented signature to `discover(string $path, string $namespace)` in all three mirrors at line 52.

confirmed.title: auth SKILL header says login/register/forgot NOT included + AUTH-006/007/008 'TBD', but same file marks them 'Entregue' and code ships them
confirmed.evidence: Line 27 blurb: "Authentication (login/registro/forgot-password) não está incluída neste pacote... Tickets AUTH-006/007/008 (TBD) preveem shipar páginas Inertia-React opt-in". This directly contradicts the same file's own Status section: line 42 "Entregue (AUTH-008)" (ForgotPasswordController/ResetPasswordController + <ForgotPasswordPage/>/<ResetPasswordPage/>), line 60 "Entregue (AUTH-006)" (LoginController/LogoutController + <LoginPage/>), line 70 "Entregue (AUTH-007)" (RegisterController/EmailVerificationController + <RegisterPage/>). Code confirms delivery: packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/{Login,Register,ForgotPassword,ResetPassword,EmailVerification}Controller.php and packages-js/auth/src/{Login,Register,ForgotPassword,ResetPassword,VerifyEmailNotice}Page.tsx all exist. AUTH-006/007/008 are no longer 'TBD'.
confirmed.fix: Rewrite the line 27 blurb to state that arqel-dev/auth now ships opt-in Inertia-React login/register/forgot-reset/verify pages (AUTH-006/007/008 delivered), enabled via Panel::login()/registration()/passwordReset(); drop the 'não está incluída' / 'TBD' framing (keep only the note that a starter kit remains an alternative).

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. packages/auth/SKILL.md:27 asserts "Authentication (login/registro/forgot-password) não está incluída neste pacote" and calls "Tickets AUTH-006/007/008 (TBD)" future work. This directly contradicts (a) the same file's own Status section — line 42 "Entregue (AUTH-008)" (ForgotPasswordController/ResetPasswordController + ForgotPasswordPage/ResetPasswordPage), line 60 "Entregue (AUTH-006)" (LoginController/LogoutController + LoginPage), line 70 "Entregue (AUTH-007)" (RegisterController/EmailVerificationController + RegisterPage/VerifyEmailNoticePage); (b) the file's own table at lines 15-25 documenting all five shipped Inertia controllers/components; and (c) the code, which I verified on disk: packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/{Login,Logout,Register,ForgotPassword,ResetPassword,EmailVerification}Controller.php and packages-js/auth/src/{LoginPage,RegisterPage,ForgotPasswordPage,ResetPasswordPage,VerifyEmailNoticePage}.tsx all exist. AUTH-006/007/008 are delivered, not TBD, and auth is not included in any swept family (R11 ai/react/ui, R12 cli/audit/widgets, R13 fields/actions) — this line was never touched. Fix: rewrite the line 27 blurb to state arqel-dev/auth now ships opt-in Inertia-React login/register/forgot-reset/verify pages (AUTH-006/007/008 delivered) enabled via Panel::login()/registration()/passwordReset(), dropping the "não está incluída"/"TBD" framing while keeping the note that a Laravel starter kit remains an alternative.

confirmed.title: authentication guide TL;DR claims Arqel 'does not currently publish login/register pages' and calls AUTH-006/007/008 'planned' — they are delivered
confirmed.evidence: Line 3 TL;DR (present tense): "Arqel does not currently publish login/register pages... An opt-in Inertia-React flow inside arqel-dev/auth is planned in tickets AUTH-006/007/008 — once shipped...". Also line 25 "(under review)", and the comparison table lines 82-90 label the column "Arqel-native (TBD)" with "Yes opt-in (planned)" for Forgot password and "Yes (planned)" for Profile page. But the arqel-dev/auth package ships all these controllers + npm pages (see packages/auth/SKILL.md 'Entregue AUTH-006/007/008' and packages/auth/src + packages-js/auth/src). Forgot/Reset password (AUTH-008) is delivered, not 'planned'.
confirmed.fix: Update the TL;DR to say Arqel now publishes opt-in bundled login/register/forgot-reset/verify Inertia-React pages via arqel-dev/auth (AUTH-006/007/008 shipped), with the starter-kit path as an alternative; change table cells from 'planned'/'TBD' to reflect delivered features (forgot-password delivered).

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. apps/docs/guide/authentication.md:3 TL;DR states in present tense that "Arqel does not currently publish login/register pages" and that the Inertia-React auth flow in arqel-dev/auth "is planned in tickets AUTH-006/007/008 — once shipped...". This directly contradicts the shipped code: packages/auth/SKILL.md marks AUTH-006, AUTH-007, and AUTH-008 as "Entregue" (Delivered), and the source confirms it — packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/ contains LoginController.php, RegisterController.php, ForgotPasswordController.php, ResetPasswordController.php, EmailVerificationController.php, LogoutController.php, and packages-js/auth/src/ ships LoginPage.tsx, RegisterPage.tsx, ForgotPasswordPage.tsx, ResetPasswordPage.tsx, VerifyEmailNoticePage.tsx. Line 20 ("under review") and the comparison table (lines 83-90) further mislabel the delivered features as "TBD"/"planned" (e.g. Forgot password "Yes opt-in (planned)" though AUTH-008 is explicitly delivered per SKILL.md; Profile page "Yes (planned)"). This is a guide file (apps/docs/guide/authentication.md) never touched by any prior swept family (which covered reference/php setters, README Artisan lists, trilingual reference drift, cloud env-vars, and unrelated SKILL.md status lists). The fix should update the TL;DR to state Arqel now publishes opt-in bundled login/register/forgot-reset/verify Inertia-React pages via arqel-dev/auth (AUTH-006/007/008 shipped), with the starter-kit path as an alternative, and change the table cells from planned/TBD to delivered.

confirmed.title: Guide says starter-kit detection in arqel:doctor is '(planned)' via AUTH-005-doctor, but the shipped DoctorCommand already performs it
confirmed.evidence: Line 128: "Starting with AUTH-005-doctor (planned), the command will detect: - Whether a starter kit is installed (Breeze, Jetstream, or Fortify) — warns when absent." But packages/core/src/Console/DoctorCommand.php (signature 'arqel:doctor', registered in ArqelServiceProvider) already implements checkAuthStarterKit() (lines 454-486) which probes Breeze/Jetstream/Fortify service providers and emits the exact 'No Laravel auth starter kit detected' warning. The starter-kit detection is delivered, not planned.
confirmed.fix: Drop the '(planned)' framing for starter-kit detection; state that arqel:doctor already checks for an installed starter kit and warns when absent. Keep only the genuinely-pending bullets (e.g., /login and /admin route registration detection, which DoctorCommand does not yet implement).

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. apps/docs/guide/authentication.md:128 says "Starting with AUTH-005-doctor (planned), the command will detect: ... Whether a starter kit is installed (Breeze, Jetstream, or Fortify) — warns when absent." But packages/core/src/Console/DoctorCommand.php already ships this: signature 'arqel:doctor' (line 45), registered in ArqelServiceProvider (line 55), the checks array invokes checkAuthStarterKit() (line 67), and the method (lines 454-486) probes Breeze/Jetstream/Fortify service providers via class_exists and emits the exact WARN message "No Laravel auth starter kit detected. Arqel does not ship login/register pages...". The starter-kit detection is delivered, not planned — the doc's "(planned)/will detect" framing contradicts the code. This is a distinct file+line contradiction in a previously-unswept file (authentication.md doctor framing); it is not covered by any of the R1-R13 swept families (setter signatures, README command lists, trilingual drift, cloud/env vars, subpath entries, or the SKILL.md items). Proposed fix: state that arqel:doctor already checks for an installed starter kit and warns when absent; keep '(planned)' only for genuinely-pending bullets like /login and /admin route registration, which DoctorCommand does not implement.
